contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 180
- Description: capoeira → Stripe → TBM: generic createLedgerCheckoutSession in GAS + checkout page details + success banner
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/12
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/13
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/16
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/capoeira/pull/17
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/114
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/115
  
  Details:
  Took over the capoeira → Stripe → TBM integration from BigModel, who had
  only built the frontend half (calling a fictional Edgar /api/checkout
  that didn't exist). End-to-end test card flow now works against the
  deployed agroverse_shop checkout GAS in Stripe test mode, with Stripe
  checkout rendering Bico Duro's photo + donor-facing details, and a
  post-payment success banner linking to the transparency ledger.
  
  Architecture decision: do NOT route session creation through Edgar.
  Per STRIPE_LEDGER_ROUTING.md Flow 4 and how agroverse_shop's own cart
  checkout works, the canonical pattern is Frontend → Google Apps Script
  → Stripe → user pays → webhook → Edgar (logs to Stripe Social Media
  Checkout ID tab) → GAS sales_sync routes by [LEDGER_ID] prefix. Edgar
  only handles the webhook side; GAS owns session creation.
  
  Shipped:
  
  1. TrueSightDAO agroverse_shop_checkout GAS — created generic
     createLedgerCheckoutSession action accepting (ledger, amount,
     currency, description, details, image_url, environment, success_url,
     cancel_url, source). Generic for any managed ledger (TBM today,
     AGL15/SEF1/PP1 etc. future). Pushed via clasp; deployed as version
     29 after fixing a missing webapp manifest block in appsscript.json
     that broke v26 + v27 deploys (had to learn that clasp push doesn't
     auto-update the deployment URL — it serves a pinned version).
  
  2. TrueSightDAO/capoeira#12 — rewired capoeira-checkout.js from
     BigModel's fictional Edgar endpoint to the GAS action. Env-aware
     (localhost → development → Stripe test, prod → live).
  
  3. TrueSightDAO/capoeira#13 — fixed a single-character typo in the
     GAS URL (case-sensitive; mine was Nwe should have been nWe).
  
  4. TrueSightDAO/capoeira#16 — pass image_url (Bico Duro profile) +
     details (donor-facing explanation) to the GAS action so Stripe
     Checkout shows context, not just a bare line item. Used the apex
     agroverse.shop domain (not www.) because Stripe doesn't follow
     redirects when fetching product images.
  
  5. TrueSightDAO/capoeira#17 — donation-success banner on index.html
     surfaced after Stripe's success_url redirect. Thanks message,
     transparency-dashboard link, session_id as reference. URL scrubbed
     via history.replaceState so refresh doesn't re-fire.
  
  6. TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context#114 — STRIPE_LEDGER_ROUTING.md
     Flow 4 reframed from PROPOSED to shipped: generic managed-ledger
     Stripe inflows (not donation-specific). Documents the GAS action
     signature.
  
  7. TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context#115 — added unified Mermaid
     overview diagram at the top of STRIPE_LEDGER_ROUTING.md showing all
     5 entry points + 2 session-creation paths + shared webhook flow +
     2 routing forks + transparency surface. Color-coded with legend.
  
  Live verification: Gary completed a real Stripe test donation
  (cs_test_a1vwNOiOx5B0HNTDZr7ANLiaxsIl5FaCh1Un8E92da3CVjvVNR5mzdlBq7),
  confirmed the photo + details render on Stripe's hosted page, and
  confirmed the success banner with transparency link displays after
  redirect.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 300.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------
